claims.claim: To synchronize pages, you need Read and Synchronize page permissions
claims.evidence: WebPageAclRolePermissionEvaluator.cs line 90: 'return grantedPermissions.Contains(WebPageAclPermissions.READ) && grantedPermissions.Contains(permission);' — for non-individually-checked permissions (Synchronize is NOT in IndividuallyCheckedPermissions per WebsiteConstants.cs line 20-24), both READ and the requested permission must be granted. WebPageAclPermissions.cs line 17: READ = "Read"; line 41: SYNCHRONIZE = "Synchronize". WebPageCommandManagerBase.cs line 280 and 493 confirm SYNCHRONIZE is the permission checked during content sync operations.

claims.claim: Link text 'page permissions' is descriptive per REC-005
claims.location: Section: Pages (under Synchronize content), sentence 2 — linkText="page permissions"
claims.evidence: REC-005 recommends descriptive link text that tells the reader what they'll find. 'page permissions' accurately describes the destination content (Page permission management page). REC-005's applicableTo is [edit-and-publish-pages, workflows], not content-sync specifically, but the underlying principle applies universally and no invariants are violated.
claims.notes: REC-005 extended slightly beyond its listed applicableTo scope. The principle is sound and no invariant conflict exists.

researchRecommendationChecks.notes: Writer used descriptive link text 'page permissions' instead of vague text. Consistent with recommendation to 'Write link text that makes sense without the surrounding text.' Single cross-reference placement at the most relevant location (Pages subsection).

domainInsightChecks.notes: Multi-layer permission architecture is correctly represented — the sentence states 'Read and Synchronize page permissions' which maps to the ACL layer. Application-layer permission (Access channel) is implied via the channel context but not restated, which is appropriate for a cross-reference sentence that links to the full details page.
Business-user page uses domain-specific interaction verbs. 'synchronize' and 'you need' are appropriate business-user vocabulary. No stale icon references introduced.
